title: 8 Wires User Management
body_text:
8	Wires User Management
8.1	Overview of Wires Access and Administration
To access Wires, a Wires User must have:
A Client Centre User ID with access to the Wires application. Complete this step first because the individualâs Client Centre ID is required in MTS.
An MTS user ID assigned the applicable permissions. See Section 8.6, Creating a Wires User in MTS.
Note:	Authorized Wires users must have a 2-Step Security token assigned to their Client Centre profile to access the Pending Incoming and Pending Outgoing options on the navigation menu. See The User Management Part for information on assigning a 2-Step Security Token.
8.2	Granting Wires Access to a Client Centre User or User Management Security Officer
See the User Management Part for procedures on granting Wires access to Client Centre Users and User Management Security Officers.
8.3	Overview of Wires User Management
MTS Administrators use the Money Transfer System (MTS) to manage Wires Usersâ permissions. Wires Users must have a Client Centre ID with access to the Wires Application. See Section 8.2, Granting Wires Access to a Client Centre User or User Management Security Officer.
Note:	Wires Users donât have access to the MTS application.
MTS Administrators can add, modify, and delete Wires Users and assign Wires permissions to:
Create outgoing wires
Approve outgoing wires
Accept or return incoming wires
Manage outgoing wire templates that are pending deletion
View and manage foreign exchange spreads and outgoing wire fees
Note:	Access to these functions is based on the userâs permissions.
8.4	Description of Queues in the MTS User Tab
The following queues display on the User tab. See Figure 9.
User Queue
â Select
User
to view Wires Users.
Admin Queue
â Select
Admin
to view MTS Administrators.
Change List Queue
â Select
Change List
to authorize or delete pending user and administrator changes.
Figure 9: Queues in the User Tab
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
8.5	Description of Columns in the MTS User Queue
The following table describes each column in the User queue. See Figure 10.
Column Name
Description
Transit
The Wires Userâs home transit number.
User ID
The MTS user ID of the Wires User. Must be the same as the Client Centre User ID.
Name
The Wires Userâs name.
Daily Limit
The maximum value of outgoing wires the Wires User can approve.
Transfer Limit
The maximum value of an outgoing wire the Wires User can approve.
Committed
The total amount of outgoing wires the Wires User approved for the day. The amount is reset to zero at the end of the business day.
Balance
The daily limit subtracted from the committed amount.
Figure 10: Columns in User Queue
Note:	Limits apply to Wires User assigned the âAuthorize Outgoing Transfersâ permission. Limits are in Canadian dollars. Wires created in other currencies are compared to the userâs limits but at the Canadian equivalent amount.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
8.6	Creating a Wires User in MTS
The Wires processing model your financial institution selects influences the Wires Usersâ home transit number and permissions. See Chapter 5, Selecting a Wires Processing Model, for a description of centralized, partially centralized, and decentralized Wires environments.
An MTS Administrator creates a Wires User as follows:
Ensure that the new Wires User is set up in User Management and is assigned access to the Wires application. See Section 8.2, Granting Wires Access to a Client Centre User or User Management Security Officer.
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, then select
New
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Create New User
Complete the Wires Userâs profile as follows. See Figure 12.
â
Enter the MTS user ID. It must be the same as the Client Centre User ID. Donât exceed 20 characters.
â¡
Select the userâs home transit number from the drop-down menu.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
â¢
Select the check box if the Wires User is authorized to access branches that are linked to their home transit number.
â£
Select the check box to remove the checkmark and activate the profile.
â¤
Enter the Wires Userâs name and contact information.
â¥
Enter the maximum value of an outgoing wire the user can approve (if applicable).
Important
:	Users responsible for approving wires created in
MemberDirect
Â®
Business Services (MDB) or Forge Commercial must have sufficient limits to approve wires for all business customers.
â¦
Enter the maximum value of outgoing wires the user can approve in a business day (if applicable).
Important
:	These limits display in Canadian dollars. Wires converts the amount of an outgoing wire in foreign funds (including US dollars) to Canadian dollars at the live rate to compare the wire amount to the Level 2 Authorization limit. If live rates arenât available, Wires uses the last available rate. This conversion only determines the number of approvals required for foreign funds outgoing wires and doesnât affect the currency of the outgoing wire.
Important
:	Users responsible for approving wires created in MDB or Forge Commercial must have sufficient limits to approve wires for all business customers.
Note:	The Transfer Limit Override and Daily Limit Override fields donât apply in the Wires application. Leave blank.
â§
Select the check box beside the appropriate Wires User permission(s):
â
View Incoming Transfers
â
Select the check box if the Wires User is authorized to view incoming wires.
â
Update Incoming Transfers
â
Select the check box if the Wires User is authorized to accept or return incoming wires.
â
View Outgoing Transfers
â
Select the check the box if the Wires User is authorized to view outgoing wires.
â
Create Outgoing Transfers
â
Select the check box if the Wires User is authorized to create outgoing wires and templates.
â
Authorize Outgoing Transfers
â
Select the check box if the Wires User is authorized to approve outgoing wires.
â
Permanently Delete Templates
â Select the check box if the Wires User is authorized to manage wire templates that are pending deletion.
â
Spreads and Fees
â Select the check box if the Wires User is authorized to manage foreign exchange spreads and outgoing wire fees for your financial institution.
Note:	The Central User Permissions section is for Central 1âs use only.
Select
Submit
.
Figure 12: Completing the Wires User MTS Profile
Have another Administrator authorize the new user. See Section 8.9, Authorizing Changes to Wires Usersâ Profiles in MTS.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
8.7	Modifying a Wires User in MTS
An MTS Administrator modifies a Wires Userâs MTS permissions, contact information, and limits as follows:
Note: 	You canât change the Wires Userâs user ID and home transit number.
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS.
Select a branch from the Transit Number drop-down menu, then select
Go
. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Select Branch
Note:	Select
All
in the drop-down menu to view users for all branches.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
Select the user ID of the Wires User you want to modify. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Selecting a Wires User
Tip
:	If there is a pending change to the Wires Userâs profile, it wonât appear in the User queue. Authorize or cancel the pending change before proceeding. See Section 8.8, Authorizing Changes to Wires Usersâ Profiles in MTS.
Modify the user as required. See Section 8.6, Creating a Wires User in MTS, for a description of each field.
Select
Update
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Modify User
Have another MTS Administrator authorize the change. See Section 8.9, Authorizing Changes to Wires Usersâ Profiles in MTS.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
8.8	Deleting a Wires User in MTS
An MTS Administrator deletes a Wires User as follows:
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS.
Select a branch from the transit number drop-down menu, then select
Go
. See Figure 16.
Note:	Select
All
in the drop-down menu to view users for all branches.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
Figure 16: Selecting a Branch
Select the user ID of the Wires User you want to delete. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Selecting User ID
Tip
:	If there is a pending change to the Wires Userâs profile, it wonât appear in the User queue. Cancel the pending change before proceeding. See Section 8.9, Authorizing Changes to Wires Usersâ Profiles in MTS.
Select
Delete
. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Delete User
Have another MTS Administrator authorize the change via the Change List. See Section 8.9, Authorizing Changes to Wires Usersâ Profiles in MTS.
Have a User Management Security Officer to remove the Wires application from the userâs Client Centre profile or delete their profile if Client Centre Access is no longer required (e.g., no longer your employee). See the User Management Part for procedures.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
8.9	Authorizing Changes to Wires Usersâ Profiles in MTS
When you add, modify, or delete a Wires Userâs MTS profile, a second MTS Administrator must authorize the change.
Note:	See Section 7.4, Description of Columns in the MTS Change List Queue (Users and Administrators), for a description of each column.
To Authorize changes to a Wireâs User profile in MTS:
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, and select
Change List
. See Figure 19.
Tip
:	The number next to âUser Changesâ reflects the number of unauthorized user and administrator changes.
Figure 19: Access Change List
Select the user ID of the Wires User you want to authorize. See Figure 20.
Figure 20: Select User ID
Review the change, then select
Authorize
. See Figure 21. Alternatively, select
Cancel
to delete the change.
Figure 21: Authorize Change
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
END
